title: Distributed Coordination and Agreement Protocols
description: A flowchart showing the logical progression and relationships between core protocols used for achieving coordination and agreement in a distributed system.
elements: Mutual Exclusion (e.g., Token Ring, Central Server)
relationships: A Client Request often requires coordination to be handled correctly. Leader Election is a prerequisite for many protocols to establish a coordinator. Mutual Exclusion ensures exclusive access to a shared resource. Consensus is the fundamental problem of getting processes to agree on a value, enabling protocols like Atomic Broadcast, which ensures all processes deliver the same messages in the same order. The outcome is a consistent System State or Decision.
